sx-962c889681: `**` treated as single `*` (matched only one subdomain level)
sx-c0f5ea5d31: network policies selected endpoints across all clusters
network policies select endpoints from local cluster only by default
sx-f8b8fa4436: load balancer ip pool api version
sx-3c27ce6268: save values to file and pass via -f
sx-bf8501652c: loadbalancer/clusterip traffic accepted regardless of destination port
loadbalancer/clusterip traffic dropped if destination port doesn't match provisioned service (north-south direction)
sx-6ce8889c1b: ciliumnetworkpolicy cannot match reserved:init labels; use ciliumclusterwidenetworkpolicy instead
sx-e72940b5dc: cluster name and id are automatically inferred for external workloads
cluster name and id are no longer automatically inferred; must be explicitly specified
sx-bff14b3ff1: enable-endpoint-routes now automatically sets enable-local-node-route to false
sx-41dbef8a42: l7 visibility can be configured via pod annotation policy.cilium.io/proxy-visibility
l7 visibility via pod annotations is no longer supported; use l7 policies instead
sx-e26bfa7f6b: gateway api v1beta1 resources (gatewayclass, gateway, httproute) are supported
gateway api v1beta1 resources are no longer supported; use v1 crds and resources
sx-67cba03a98: tunnel protocol is automatically set to geneve in native routing mode with dsr+geneve enabled
tunnel protocol is no longer automatically set; must be explicitly configured
sx-cb80375746: bpf masquerade silently falls back to iptables when requirements aren't met
bpf masquerade fails initialization with error logging when requirements aren't met
sx-18a59bc039: enable-k8s-event-handover and cnp-status-update-interval options are available
enable-k8s-event-handover and cnp-status-update-interval options are removed
sx-4765754466: structured definition decoupling repository and tag
sx-276b2f6f95: prometheus metrics disabled by default for cilium-operator and clustermesh kvstore
sx-586c0f2c73: default zero, honoring upstream dns ttls
sx-7a22240104: matching traffic allowed without rerouting when no gateway nodes available
matching traffic dropped when no gateway nodes available
sx-13ac321707: agents did not auto-cleanup stale meshed cluster information after kvstore reconnection
agents auto-cleanup stale meshed cluster information after kvstore reconnection
sx-b9b86a3345: deny policies did not always take precedence over allow policies; cidr-based deny could be overridden by overlapping allow policies
deny policies now always take precedence over allow policies; cidr-based deny blocks overlapping allow policies
sx-559164604c: cilium_host ipv6 assigned from native host interface
cilium_host ipv6 assigned from ipam pool
sx-a9180dc066: securitycontext now applies to container; use podsecuritycontext for pod-level
sx-cd8a96eaeb: kernel network interface (kni) library and driver
sx-33bfa5b41c: explicit sm2 xform parameter in asymmetric xform
sx-965e4a6f5c: defaults to 40 bytes when rss_key is set
user must provide rss_key_len when rss_key != null
sx-edeeef1af0: moved to internal headers, not visible to applications
sx-d75ff06bc1: rejects key-only input, handles only key=value
sx-9d38ca8575: bcc's built-in clang compiler that compiles bpf code at runtime
sx-a184364981: bcc's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints
sx-45e4faae89: bcc's code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments
sx-15c08a1950: bcc's semi-object-oriented c code style for working with bpf maps
sx-8a0092bbd3: bcc requiring manual boilerplate in user-space to set up bpf programs
sx-0122d5a55a: bcc requiring kernel headers package installed on every target host
sx-21fb850364: bcc detecting compilation errors only at runtime after restarting the application
sx-716b632d0d: bcc unconditionally sets locked memory limit to infinity
libbpf does not automatically set locked memory limit
sx-f63363659f: libbpf_set_print(print_libbpf_log) with function returning int and taking (enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args)
libbpf_set_print callback now returns void instead of int
sx-31d4813619: bcc macros like bpf_array, bpf_hash, bpf_percpu_array
sx-3b5e006fd1: libbpf requires explicit map size specification
sx-ad54679d29: libbpf helper function calls (bpf_map_operation_elem(&some_map, some, args))
sx-f2acfaadb4: bcc auto-detects program type without sec()
libbpf requires sec() macro with specific section name
sx-9b5e8e7078: bcc may handle key/value types implicitly
libbpf requires explicit key_size/value_size for perf_event_array, stack_trace, devmap, cpumap
sx-e37af00a8c: global variables could be left uninitialized
global variables must be initialized (even to zero)
sx-604c82a3d9: read-only globals could be declared without const volatile
read-only globals must use const volatile to prevent compiler optimizations
sx-0987ee15b2: mutable globals could be declared static
mutable globals must not be marked as static
sx-faaa575e18: declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code connects them
declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code creates independent, unconnected variables
share variable between bpf and userspace
sx-b4fbdc20da: loops in bpf code could be used without explicit unrolling pragma on older kernels
loops in bpf code must be marked with #pragma unroll unless targeting 5.3+ kernel
sx-e241259efd: static functions in bpf code must be marked __always_inline
static functions can drop __always_inline (kernel 4.16+); consider using __noinline instead
sx-b8d3b9ab2c: explicit struct with __uint(type, bpf_map_type_hash) annotated sec(".maps")
sx-770c995121: explicit sec("...") annotation on every program
sx-108575f501: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()
sx-494dfc1fa8: show all advertised paths including non-best paths regardless of addpath
show all advertised paths including non-best paths only if addpath is enabled
sx-a996daabf4: ospfv3 full adjacency persisted despite mtu mismatch
ospfv3 no longer maintains full adjacency when mtu mismatch is detected
sx-f266ab9c2d: keywords could be used as interface names in static route configuration
keywords are rejected as interface names
sx-4784b10da5: pim designated router (dr) did not change when the router's address changed
pim dr now changes when the router's address changes
sx-e12d2ebb7f: segment-routing/traffic-eng no longer appears in configuration unless explicitly configured
sx-a2c8ccacef: ip rip send/receive version command behavior
ip rip send/receive version command behavior fixed
sx-22c0ce0d91: ospf installed routes for directly attached networks
ospf no longer installs routes for directly attached networks
sx-445d899127: pim sparse-dense mode could treat a group as dense even with an rp configured
pim sparse-dense mode now treats a group as sparse if an rp is configured
sx-61826f5906: bgpd did not allow overriding remote-as per-neighbor
bgpd now allows overriding remote-as per-neighbor
sx-0654630fce: replace-as did not replace the actual local-as when used with confederation
replace-as now replaces the actual local-as when used with confederation
sx-93b9b215b3: 'no activate' was not honored for dynamic neighbors in a peer-group
'no activate' is now honored for dynamic neighbors in a peer-group
sx-b86b17be5b: bgp route acceptance when confederation as matches peer as
bgp now does not reject the route if confederation as matches peer as
accept bgp route with confederation as
sx-4cb4eb72d4: ospf nssa routers could originate type-5 lsas directly
ospf nssa external route not advertised
sx-600640722c: bmp updates prepended local-as to as_path
bmp updates no longer prepend local-as to as_path
sx-ddbed443f2: show bfd peers brief json output identical to show bfd peers
show bfd peers brief json output different from show bfd peers
sx-6c3315a470: llgr stale routes were advertised to non-llgr peers
llgr stale routes are not advertised to non-llgr peers
sx-71c4d158c3: if multiple mpls labels were received, only the bottom label was used
up to 10 labels are supported per prefix
sx-23b3a27a50: confederation asns were placed into as-set
confederation asns are no longer placed into as-set, left as alone
sx-bac0ac8e5c: 'segment-routing ipv6' enabled by default when 'no segment-routing ipv6' is configured
srv6-only command now correctly defaults when 'no segment-routing ipv6' is set
sx-f2261bf26b: remotetransposedsid field in json output of vpn paths
sx-464cb60a5d: bfd hold timer started regardless of hold-time value
bfd hold timer only starts if hold-time is configured (not zero)
sx-21dfa67313: rip sends updates on disabled networks
rip does not send updates on disabled networks
sx-4ade30ea05: zebra accepts any ra lifetime and interval values
zebra now checks ra lifetime and interval for validity
sx-818df7a81e: ospfd allows virtual link creation on non-abr routers
ospfd now correctly rejects virtual link creation on non-abr routers
sx-2ef9c6b1d2: zebra cli commands for max-bw and related settings lack 'no' versions
zebra cli now has 'no' versions for max-bw and others
sx-d9b42417ab: sending software-version capability even when explicitly disabled
not sending software-version capability when explicitly disabled
sx-30da675eba: rip/ripng default flush timer was changed in a prior version
rip/ripng default flush timer reverted to previous value
sx-1486d39b40: enabled by default for datacenter profile
sx-0746dc15a2: specified rd is no longer overridden
sx-f78a8a8f37: malformed packets between ibgp peers always reset the session
malformed packets are handled the same way as for ebgp (withdrawing or discarding)
sx-f163546e2e: bmp does not send end-of-rib by default
sx-253036cca9: bgp opens a listening port for vrf instances
bgp does not open a listening port for vrf instances
sx-945f8a7a32: soft-reconfiguration processing stalls bestpath processing until complete
soft-reconfiguration no longer stalls bestpath processing
sx-c7ee712d0f: irdp (icmp router discovery protocol) code in zebra
sx-710a4dc6ff: setting extcommunity link bandwidth value to zero was not allowed or had no effect
setting extcommunity link bandwidth value to zero is now allowed
sx-07343c0d77: `copy config from file` did not actually apply the config
`copy config from file` now actually applies the config
sx-76aed9ba0c: evpn vni processing delay is now 10ms
sx-f12b2cc010: bgp gr used keepalive message as implicit eor
bgp graceful restart end of rib
sx-c6ca007498: bgp session could start without a bgp identifier being set
bgp session will not start if bgp identifier is not set
sx-164f1beb99: `set evpn gateway-ip ipv4` and `set evpn gateway-ip ipv6` route-map commands worked as before
sx-ec66482f80: ipv4 class e addresses (240.0.0.0/4) were not treated as connected routes
ipv4 class e addresses (240.0.0.0/4) are now used as connected routes by default
sx-f5cb263b78: system routes were selectable via 'ip protocol' route map match
sx-0e7d0147e9: likely corrected form of the command
sx-120598e375: bgp aggregate routes were advertised to contributing ases
bgp aggregate routes are no longer advertised to contributing ases
stop aggregate leaking to source AS
sx-29c107f962: `show bgp <afi> <safi>` no longer displays peers in groups
show bgp afi safi format changed
sx-2797ce8fc7: rtadv remains enabled when bgp instance is unconfigured
rtadv is disabled when bgp instance is unconfigured
sx-3b790f50a1: node-len could not be configured as 0
node-len can now be configured as 0
sx-7bc8fbae00: ip[v6] forwarding was configured via legacy mechanism
ip[v6] forwarding is now managed via nb/mgmtd
sx-eae71b28ca: router alert configuration was optional for pim
router alert configuration is now required for pim
sx-a4da333fed: v6 router advertisements were sent after interface up-down-up
v6 router advertisements are not sent anymore after interface up-down-up
ra not sent after interface flap
sx-3da0a0a994: gracefulrestartcapability json field in bgp output
bgp json graceful restart field missing
sx-f70e188922: per-daemon config files (e.g. bgpd.conf) were updated when 'write memory' was issued
per-daemon config files are no longer updated when 'write memory' is issued
config not saved after write memory
sx-76450ec3fb: sec("xdp1") or sec("xdp_prog1") style random suffixes
sx-3f478b8fd0: section names to uniquely identify bpf programs
c function names to uniquely identify bpf programs
sx-b30ba1c7f4: bpf program names used for pinning
sx-814ba55387: return negative -exxx directly and set positive errno to exxx
sx-568166ab0f: null pointers caused crashes in destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free()
safe to pass NULL to close
sx-ee02c7ee8d: pointer-returning apis (e.g. bpf_object__open_file, btf__parse, perf_buffer__new) return special non-null error pointers (err_ptr encoding error code)
pointer-returning apis return null on error, with errno set to the error code
sx-2c0cff3e19: libbpf_get_error() is the recommended way to check pointer-returning apis for errors
sx-9e64f11018: return -1 on error with errno set
sx-4fe2e14fc6: libbpf returned -1 on error and set errno
libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly and also sets errno
sx-5f62c8744a: high-level apis returned error codes directly but did not set errno
high-level apis now also set errno with a positive error code
sx-6486587a55: return -1 with errno set, or return -exxx without setting errno
return -errno directly and still set errno
sx-0b22698ad6: non-conforming section names logged a warning
non-conforming section names cause hard errors
sx-96da8a4ea0: return err_ptr-encoded error on failure (non-null pointer with encoded error)
return null on failure, errno set to exxx
sx-fa4d91c005: allowing extra text beyond program type, e.g. 'xdp_my_prog' or 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'
only allow program type (and optional subtype after '/'), no extra parameters, e.g. 'xdp' not 'xdp_my_prog', 'cgroup_skb/ingress' not 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'
sx-6d2f03e520: uses bpf program's c function name for filename part
sx-11de4fd81b: merged into .data map, accessed via skel->data
sx-5e01cf013b: btf ext function info record size
sx-7c66db5a10: struct btf_dedup_opts (non-opts struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields)
opts-based struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size dropped
sx-9aca8ca0ff: btf__dedup() accepting new opts-based struct with btf_ext supplied through opts
sx-8589043f1e: struct btf_dump_opts (non-opts) and btf_dump__new() accepting old struct
opts-based struct btf_dump_opts and new version of btf_dump__new() accepting opts
sx-2678eab1dc: store private data on BPF object
sx-769182ebcb: store private data on BPF program
sx-1022c0fbba: store private data on BPF map
sx-9649465ca8: get xdp program id from link
sx-07b0331521: bpf_printk() now attempts to use static global functions
sx-cfe28d426a: libbpf added bpf_f_mmapable flag for all data maps
libbpf only adds bpf_f_mmapable flag for data maps with global (non-static) vars
sx-e5f9f9b53a: libbpf modified/transformed bpf verifier log level and flags before passing to kernel
libbpf passes bpf verifier log level and flags to kernel as-is
sx-67bb326102: sec("struct_ops") and sec("struct_ops.link") auto-create struct_ops maps by default
sec("?.struct_ops") and sec("?.struct_ops.link") default to not auto-create struct_ops map
sx-02467f3907: kfunc relocation resolved using exact function name including trailing underscores
triple-underscore suffix (___.*) is ignored when resolving kfunc relocations
sx-dc078edafc: get map key value type ids
sx-5a06c2faac: btf ext line info record size
sx-031ea8e619: attach functions work with any struct xdp_program object
attach functions only work with struct xdp_program objects that have not yet been loaded into the kernel
sx-5d8fe187d2: can attach multiple programs incrementally to an interface that already has an xdp program
if kernel lacks incremental attach support (pre-5.10), the only way to run multiple programs is to attach them all at once with xdp_program__attach_multi()
sx-37014a2ebc: could be used without privileges if an af_xdp socket was created beforehand and passed to a non-privileged process
still requires privileges for af_xdp socket creation
sx-318bca5b4f: could use addr straight from the rx descriptor to get packet data
must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() because the offset is carried in the upper 16 bits of addr
sx-d1d47705cb: poll() was the only way to check if wakeup is needed
xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup() returns true, you must call recvmsg(), sendto(), or poll() depending on direction
sx-6c0a593a01: af_xdp apis from xsk.h in libbpf
sx-11512b2706: xsk.h af_xdp functions (xsk_umem__fd, xsk_socket__fd, xsk_setup_xdp_prog, xsk_socket__update_xskmap)
